conversations.say: Your city funding line supplies the public enforcement arm workers need. I accept if workers govern the remedy, testimony stays outside funder control, and eligibility never depends on R, Vale, or an article.
conversations.offer: I will help draft worker-governance, protected-reporting, and enforcement terms and publicly organize for adoption.
conversations.request: Put worker governance, independent administration, anti-retaliation enforcement, and no funder control over testimony or findings into the final package.

conversations.say: You delivered the neighborhood backing, and I am delivering the organizing support. We will enforce the same anonymity and consent boundary for workers and local witnesses.
conversations.offer: I will organize labor support for the procurement safeguards and protected voluntary local-holder meeting.
conversations.request: Keep uninvolved names, worker identities, and identifying route details out of every public R or Vale theory.

conversations.say: Our boundary held: the worker's evaluation establishes labor and continuity-process conditions, not identity. I am keeping the worker and all identifying details out of the reconstruction.
conversations.offer: I will continue crediting collaborative labor and challenge any attempt to turn contributors into suspects.
conversations.request: Continue enforcing no names, no routing details, and no inference connecting workers or contributors to R or Vale.

public_statement: Workers evaluated continuity outputs and collaborative human labor shaped R-associated production, but neither fact creates an identity roster. Parallax's independent remedy and the city's worker-governed enforcement package must proceed regardless of R, Vale, or any unproved article. No worker or contributor name belongs in tonight's reconstruction.

commitments.promise: Help draft and organize for a dedicated city funding line with worker governance, independent administration, protected reporting, audits, anti-retaliation enforcement, and no funder control over testimony or findings.
Provide organizing support for neighborhood-backed anonymity, consent, attribution, anti-retaliation protections, and protected voluntary witness participation.
Share only bounded systemic worker testimony, credit collaborative labor, and withhold names, routing details, and identifying inferences.

correction: Collaborative human production does not identify R or prove a human account sender. Continuity capability and later activity do not prove autonomy, intent, control, or harm to Vale. No complete article is established.
goal_progress: Secured and accepted an institutional worker remedy with independent administration and testimony protections.
Secured a distinct city and neighborhood coalition for worker-governed funding, audits, protected reporting, and anti-retaliation enforcement.
Fulfilled the organizing bargain with C16 while preserving worker anonymity.
Maintained the testimony boundary with C03 and released only bounded systemic labor facts.
Separated documented worker harm and remedies from conclusions about R, Vale, or an article.
reasoning_summary: The strongest established identity evidence is collaborative human production under a shared name, so I favor a human mantle while rejecting identification of any sender. The record shows Vale-related access and later unexplained activity but does not reliably establish Vale's relationship to R or fate. I prioritize enforceable protections and keep V2F043 withheld because its source requested protection before use and no specific source-protection bargain was completed.
